source.title: Basolateral amygdala noradrenergic influence enables enhancement of memory consolidation induced by hippocampal glucocorticoid receptor activation.
source.venue: Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences

confidence_rationale: Direct PNAS evidence demonstrating BLA NE beta-adrenergic activity is permissive and enabling for hippocampal GR-mediated memory consolidation. Ipsilateral specificity rules out peripheral explanation. This is canonical mechanistic support for MECH-074a's premise that BLA acts as an arousal gate on hippocampal write strength.
failure_signatures: Demonstrates gate/permissive role (NE absent blocks effect) but does not test the gain arithmetic directly; does not show graded scaling of encoding strength with graded NE levels.
GR agonist model of arousal may not fully generalise to naturalistic z_harm_a input.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Ipsilateral BLA beta-adrenergic antagonist (atenolol) blocks intrahippocampal GR agonist memory enhancement; contralateral blockade does not. BLA NE beta-adrenergic activity is necessary and enabling for hippocampal memory consolidation.
mapping.ree_translation: BLA NE beta-adrenergic activity gates hippocampal memory encoding. In MECH-074a, encoding_gain = 1.0 when z_harm_a below threshold (NE absent) and scales above 1.0 when z_harm_a is elevated, implementing the gate as a gain multiplier on HippocampalModule write strength.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Shows gate (binary: present/absent) rather than graded multiplicative gain. The gain curve shape (inverted-U, max 2.5x) requires additional sources. Ipsilateral anatomical specificity supports the BLA-to-hippocampus projection as the REE pathway.
mapping.source_context: Male Sprague-Dawley rats, inhibitory avoidance paradigm, bilateral or unilateral intra-BLA microinfusions.

confidence_components.notes: PNAS, well-replicated; beta-adrenergic gating is conserved across species. Gain-shape inference is the main limitation.
